root_text.an5.76:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco yodhājīvo asicammaṁ gahetvā dhanukalāpaṁ sannayhitvā viyūḷhaṁ saṅgāmaṁ otarati.
root_text.an5.76:1.5: Tamenaṁ ussahantaṁ vāyamantaṁ pare hananti pariyāpādenti.
root_text.an5.76:1.7: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭhamo yodhājīvo santo saṁvijjamāno lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.76:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco yodhājīvo asicammaṁ gahetvā dhanukalāpaṁ sannayhitvā viyūḷhaṁ saṅgāmaṁ otarati.
root_text.an5.76:2.3: Tamenaṁ ussahantaṁ vāyamantaṁ pare upalikkhanti, tamenaṁ apanenti;
root_text.an5.76:2.5: So ñātakehi nīyamāno appatvāva ñātake antarāmagge kālaṁ karoti.
root_text.an5.76:2.7: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyo yodhājīvo santo saṁvijjamāno lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.76:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco yodhājīvo asicammaṁ gahetvā dhanukalāpaṁ sannayhitvā viyūḷhaṁ saṅgāmaṁ otarati.
root_text.an5.76:3.3: Tamenaṁ ussahantaṁ vāyamantaṁ pare upalikkhanti, tamenaṁ apanenti;
root_text.an5.76:3.6: So ñātakehi upaṭṭhahiyamāno paricariyamāno teneva ābādhena kālaṁ karoti.
root_text.an5.76:3.8: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, tatiyo yodhājīvo santo saṁvijjamāno lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.76:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco yodhājīvo asicammaṁ gahetvā dhanukalāpaṁ sannayhitvā viyūḷhaṁ saṅgāmaṁ otarati.
root_text.an5.76:4.3: Tamenaṁ ussahantaṁ vāyamantaṁ pare upalikkhanti, tamenaṁ apanenti;
root_text.an5.76:4.6: So ñātakehi upaṭṭhahiyamāno paricariyamāno vuṭṭhāti tamhā ābādhā.
root_text.an5.76:4.8: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, catuttho yodhājīvo santo saṁvijjamāno lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.76:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco yodhājīvo asicammaṁ gahetvā dhanukalāpaṁ sannayhitvā viyūḷhaṁ saṅgāmaṁ otarati.
root_text.an5.76:5.2: So taṁ saṅgāmaṁ abhivijinitvā vijitasaṅgāmo tameva saṅgāmasīsaṁ ajjhāvasati.
root_text.an5.76:5.4: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, pañcamo yodhājīvo santo saṁvijjamāno lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.76:5.5: Ime kho, bhikkhave, pañca yodhājīvā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.76:6.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcime yodhājīvūpamā puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā bhikkhūsu.
root_text.an5.76:6.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā upanissāya viharati.
root_text.an5.76:6.4: So pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya tameva gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya pavisati arakkhiteneva kāyena arakkhitāya vācāya arakkhitena cittena anupaṭṭhitāya satiyā asaṁvutehi indriyehi.
root_text.an5.76:6.5: So tattha passati mātugāmaṁ dunnivatthaṁ vā duppārutaṁ vā.
root_text.an5.76:6.6: Tassa taṁ mātugāmaṁ disvā dunnivatthaṁ vā duppārutaṁ vā rāgo cittaṁ anuddhaṁseti.
root_text.an5.76:6.7: So rāgānuddhaṁsitena cittena sikkhaṁ apaccakkhāya dubbalyaṁ anāvikatvā methunaṁ dhammaṁ paṭisevati.
root_text.an5.76:7.1: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, yodhājīvo asicammaṁ gahetvā dhanukalāpaṁ sannayhitvā viyūḷhaṁ saṅgāmaṁ otarati, so tasmiṁ saṅgāme ussahati vāyamati, tamenaṁ ussahantaṁ vāyamantaṁ pare hananti pariyāpādenti; tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi. Evarūpopi, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo hoti.
root_text.an5.76:7.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭhamo yodhājīvūpamo puggalo santo saṁvijjamāno bhikkhūsu.
root_text.an5.76:8.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā upanissāya viharati.
root_text.an5.76:8.2: So pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya tameva gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya pavisati arakkhiteneva kāyena arakkhitāya vācāya arakkhitena cittena anupaṭṭhitāya satiyā asaṁvutehi indriyehi.
root_text.an5.76:8.3: So tattha passati mātugāmaṁ dunnivatthaṁ vā duppārutaṁ vā.
root_text.an5.76:8.4: Tassa taṁ mātugāmaṁ disvā dunnivatthaṁ vā duppārutaṁ vā rāgo cittaṁ anuddhaṁseti.
root_text.an5.76:8.5: So rāgānuddhaṁsitena cittena pariḍayhateva kāyena pariḍayhati cetasā.
root_text.an5.76:8.8: rāgapariyuṭṭhitomhi, āvuso, rāgapareto, na sakkomi brahmacariyaṁ sandhāretuṁ;
root_text.an5.76:8.10: So ārāmaṁ gacchanto appatvāva ārāmaṁ antarāmagge sikkhādubbalyaṁ āvikatvā sikkhaṁ paccakkhāya hīnāyāvattati.
root_text.an5.76:9.1: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, yodhājīvo asicammaṁ gahetvā dhanukalāpaṁ sannayhitvā viyūḷhaṁ saṅgāmaṁ otarati, so tasmiṁ saṅgāme ussahati vāyamati, tamenaṁ ussahantaṁ vāyamantaṁ pare upalikkhanti, tamenaṁ apanenti; apanetvā ñātakānaṁ nenti. So ñātakehi nīyamāno appatvāva ñātake antarāmagge kālaṁ karoti; tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi. Evarūpopi, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo hoti.
root_text.an5.76:9.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyo yodhājīvūpamo puggalo santo saṁvijjamāno bhikkhūsu.
root_text.an5.76:10.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā upanissāya viharati.
root_text.an5.76:10.2: So pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya tameva gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya pavisati arakkhiteneva kāyena arakkhitāya vācāya arakkhitena cittena anupaṭṭhitāya satiyā asaṁvutehi indriyehi.
root_text.an5.76:10.3: So tattha passati mātugāmaṁ dunnivatthaṁ vā duppārutaṁ vā.
root_text.an5.76:10.4: Tassa taṁ mātugāmaṁ disvā dunnivatthaṁ vā duppārutaṁ vā rāgo cittaṁ anuddhaṁseti.
root_text.an5.76:10.5: So rāgānuddhaṁsitena cittena pariḍayhateva kāyena pariḍayhati cetasā.
root_text.an5.76:10.8: rāgapariyuṭṭhitomhi, āvuso, rāgapareto, na sakkomi brahmacariyaṁ sandhāretuṁ;
root_text.an5.76:10.11: ‘rāgapariyuṭṭhitomhi, āvuso, rāgapareto, na sakkomi brahmacariyaṁ sandhāretuṁ;
root_text.an5.76:11.2: ‘appassādā, āvuso, kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:11.3: Aṭṭhikaṅkalūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:11.4: Maṁsapesūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:11.5: Tiṇukkūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:11.6: Aṅgārakāsūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:11.7: Supinakūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:11.8: Yācitakūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:11.9: Rukkhaphalūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:11.10: Asisūnūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:11.11: Sattisūlūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:11.12: Sappasirūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:11.14: māyasmā sikkhādubbalyaṁ āvikatvā sikkhaṁ paccakkhāya hīnāyāvattī’ti.
root_text.an5.76:12.1: So sabrahmacārīhi evaṁ ovadiyamāno evaṁ anusāsiyamāno evamāha:
root_text.an5.76:12.2: ‘kiñcāpi, āvuso, appassādā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo;
root_text.an5.76:12.3: atha kho nevāhaṁ sakkomi brahmacariyaṁ sandhāretuṁ, sikkhādubbalyaṁ āvikatvā sikkhaṁ paccakkhāya hīnāyāvattissāmī’ti.
root_text.an5.76:12.4: So sikkhādubbalyaṁ āvikatvā sikkhaṁ paccakkhāya hīnāyāvattati.
root_text.an5.76:13.1: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, yodhājīvo asicammaṁ gahetvā dhanukalāpaṁ sannayhitvā viyūḷhaṁ saṅgāmaṁ otarati, so tasmiṁ saṅgāme ussahati vāyamati, tamenaṁ ussahantaṁ vāyamantaṁ pare upalikkhanti, tamenaṁ apanenti; apanetvā ñātakānaṁ nenti, tamenaṁ ñātakā upaṭṭhahanti paricaranti. So ñātakehi upaṭṭhahiyamāno paricariyamāno teneva ābādhena kālaṁ karoti; tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi. Evarūpopi, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo hoti.
root_text.an5.76:13.2: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, tatiyo yodhājīvūpamo puggalo santo saṁvijjamāno bhikkhūsu.
root_text.an5.76:14.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā upanissāya viharati.
root_text.an5.76:14.2: So pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya tameva gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya pavisati arakkhiteneva kāyena arakkhitāya vācāya arakkhitena cittena anupaṭṭhitāya satiyā asaṁvutehi indriyehi.
root_text.an5.76:14.3: So tattha passati mātugāmaṁ dunnivatthaṁ vā duppārutaṁ vā.
root_text.an5.76:14.4: Tassa taṁ mātugāmaṁ disvā dunnivatthaṁ vā duppārutaṁ vā rāgo cittaṁ anuddhaṁseti.
root_text.an5.76:14.5: So rāgānuddhaṁsitena cittena pariḍayhateva kāyena pariḍayhati cetasā.
root_text.an5.76:14.8: rāgapariyuṭṭhitomhi, āvuso, rāgapareto, na sakkomi brahmacariyaṁ sandhāretuṁ;
root_text.an5.76:14.11: ‘rāgapariyuṭṭhitomhi, āvuso, rāgapareto, na sakkomi brahmacariyaṁ sandhāretuṁ;
root_text.an5.76:15.2: ‘appassādā, āvuso, kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:15.3: Aṭṭhikaṅkalūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:15.4: Maṁsapesūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā …pe… tiṇukkūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … aṅgārakāsūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … supinakūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … yācitakūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … rukkhaphalūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … asisūnūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … sattisūlūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā … sappasirūpamā kāmā vuttā bhagavatā bahudukkhā bahupāyāsā, ādīnavo ettha bhiyyo.
root_text.an5.76:15.6: māyasmā sikkhādubbalyaṁ āvikatvā sikkhaṁ paccakkhāya hīnāyāvattī’ti.
root_text.an5.76:16.1: So sabrahmacārīhi evaṁ ovadiyamāno evaṁ anusāsiyamāno evamāha:
root_text.an5.76:16.3: Na dānāhaṁ, āvuso, sikkhādubbalyaṁ āvikatvā sikkhaṁ paccakkhāya hīnāyāvattissāmī’ti.
root_text.an5.76:17.1: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, yodhājīvo asicammaṁ gahetvā dhanukalāpaṁ sannayhitvā viyūḷhaṁ saṅgāmaṁ otarati, so tasmiṁ saṅgāme ussahati vāyamati, tamenaṁ ussahantaṁ vāyamantaṁ pare upalikkhanti, tamenaṁ apanenti; apanetvā ñātakānaṁ nenti, tamenaṁ ñātakā upaṭṭhahanti paricaranti. So ñātakehi upaṭṭhahiyamāno paricariyamāno vuṭṭhāti tamhā ābādhā; tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an5.76:17.3: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, catuttho yodhājīvūpamo puggalo santo saṁvijjamāno bhikkhūsu.
root_text.an5.76:18.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu aññataraṁ gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā upanissāya viharati.
root_text.an5.76:18.2: So pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya tameva gāmaṁ vā nigamaṁ vā piṇḍāya pavisati rakkhiteneva kāyena rakkhitāya vācāya rakkhitena cittena upaṭṭhitāya satiyā saṁvutehi indriyehi.
root_text.an5.76:18.3: So cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā na nimittaggāhī hoti nānubyañjanaggāhī.
root_text.an5.76:18.4: Yatvādhikaraṇamenaṁ cakkhundriyaṁ asaṁvutaṁ viharantaṁ abhijjhādomanassā pāpakā akusalā dhammā anvāssaveyyuṁ, tassa saṁvarāya paṭipajjati; rakkhati cakkhundriyaṁ; cakkhundriye saṁvaraṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an5.76:18.9: manasā dhammaṁ viññāya na nimittaggāhī hoti nānubyañjanaggāhī.
root_text.an5.76:18.10: Yatvādhikaraṇamenaṁ manindriyaṁ asaṁvutaṁ viharantaṁ abhijjhādomanassā pāpakā akusalā dhammā anvāssaveyyuṁ, tassa saṁvarāya paṭipajjati; rakkhati manindriyaṁ; manindriye saṁvaraṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an5.76:18.12: vivittaṁ senāsanaṁ bhajati araññaṁ rukkhamūlaṁ pabbataṁ kandaraṁ giriguhaṁ susānaṁ vanapatthaṁ abbhokāsaṁ palālapuñjaṁ.
root_text.an5.76:18.13: So araññagato vā rukkhamūlagato vā suññāgāragato vā nisīdati pallaṅkaṁ ābhujitvā ujuṁ kāyaṁ paṇidhāya parimukhaṁ satiṁ upaṭṭhapetvā.
root_text.an5.76:18.14: So abhijjhaṁ loke pahāya …pe… so ime pañca nīvaraṇe pahāya cetaso upakkilese paññāya dubbalīkaraṇe
root_text.an5.76:18.15: vivicceva kāmehi …pe… catutthaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an5.76:19.1: So evaṁ samāhite citte parisuddhe pariyodāte anaṅgaṇe vigatūpakkilese mudubhūte kammaniye ṭhite āneñjappatte āsavānaṁ khayañāṇāya cittaṁ abhininnāmeti.
root_text.an5.76:20.1: Seyyathāpi so, bhikkhave, yodhājīvo asicammaṁ gahetvā dhanukalāpaṁ sannayhitvā viyūḷhaṁ saṅgāmaṁ otarati, so taṁ saṅgāmaṁ abhivijinitvā vijitasaṅgāmo tameva saṅgāmasīsaṁ ajjhāvasati; tathūpamāhaṁ, bhikkhave, imaṁ puggalaṁ vadāmi.
root_text.an5.76:20.3: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, pañcamo yodhājīvūpamo puggalo santo saṁvijjamāno bhikkhūsu.
root_text.an5.76:20.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, pañca yodhājīvūpamā puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā bhikkhūsū”ti.

translation_text.an5.76:1.1: “Mendicants, these five warriors are found in the world.
translation_text.an5.76:1.3: Firstly, one warrior dons his sword and shield, fastens his bow and arrows, and plunges into the thick of battle.
translation_text.an5.76:1.4: He strives and struggles in the battle,
translation_text.an5.76:1.5: but his foes kill him and finish him off.
translation_text.an5.76:1.7: This is the first warrior found in the world.
translation_text.an5.76:2.1: Furthermore, one warrior dons his sword and shield, fastens his bow and arrows, and plunges into the thick of battle.
translation_text.an5.76:2.2: He strives and struggles in the battle,
translation_text.an5.76:2.4: He’s carried off and taken to his relatives,
translation_text.an5.76:2.5: but he dies on the road before he reaches them.
translation_text.an5.76:2.7: This is the second warrior found in the world.
translation_text.an5.76:3.1: Furthermore, one warrior dons his sword and shield, fastens his bow and arrows, and plunges into the thick of battle.
translation_text.an5.76:3.2: He strives and struggles in the battle,
translation_text.an5.76:3.4: He’s carried off and taken to his relatives,
translation_text.an5.76:3.5: who nurse him and care for him.
translation_text.an5.76:3.6: But he dies of his injuries while in their care.
translation_text.an5.76:3.8: This is the third warrior found in the world.
translation_text.an5.76:4.1: Furthermore, one warrior dons his sword and shield, fastens his bow and arrows, and plunges into the thick of battle.
translation_text.an5.76:4.2: He strives and struggles in the battle,
translation_text.an5.76:4.4: He’s carried off and taken to his relatives,
translation_text.an5.76:4.5: who nurse him and care for him.
translation_text.an5.76:4.6: And while in their care, he recovers from his injuries.
translation_text.an5.76:4.8: This is the fourth warrior found in the world.
translation_text.an5.76:5.1: Furthermore, one warrior dons his sword and shield, fastens his bow and arrows, and plunges into the thick of battle.
translation_text.an5.76:5.2: He wins victory in battle, establishing himself as foremost in battle.
translation_text.an5.76:5.4: This is the fifth warrior found in the world.
translation_text.an5.76:5.5: These are the five warriors found in the world.
translation_text.an5.76:6.1: In the same way, these five individuals similar to warriors are found among the monks.
translation_text.an5.76:6.3: Firstly, a mendicant lives supported by a town or village.
translation_text.an5.76:6.4: He robes up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, enters a village or town for alms without guarding body, speech, and mind, without establishing mindfulness, and without restraining the sense faculties.
translation_text.an5.76:6.5: There he sees a lady scantily clad, with revealing clothes.
translation_text.an5.76:6.7: and, without resigning the training and declaring his inability to continue, he indulges in sex.
translation_text.an5.76:7.1: I say that this individual is like the warrior who is killed and finished off by his foes. Some individuals are like that.
translation_text.an5.76:7.2: This is the first individual similar to a warrior found among the monks.
translation_text.an5.76:8.1: Furthermore, a mendicant lives supported by a town or village.
translation_text.an5.76:8.2: He robes up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, enters a village or town for alms without guarding body, speech, and mind, without establishing mindfulness, and without restraining the sense faculties.
translation_text.an5.76:8.3: There he sees a lady scantily clad, with revealing clothes.
translation_text.an5.76:8.5: and his body and mind burn with it.
translation_text.an5.76:8.7: ‘Why don’t I visit the monastery and tell the monks:
translation_text.an5.76:8.8: “Reverends, I am overcome with lust, mired in lust. I am unable to keep up the spiritual life.
translation_text.an5.76:8.9: I declare my inability to continue training. I disavow it and will return to a lesser life.”’
translation_text.an5.76:8.10: But while traveling on the road, before he reaches the monastery he declares his inability to continue training. He disavows it and returns to a lesser life.
translation_text.an5.76:9.1: I say that they are like the warrior who is taken to his relatives for care, but he dies on the road before he reaches them. Some individuals are like that.
translation_text.an5.76:9.2: This is the second individual similar to a warrior found among the monks.
translation_text.an5.76:10.1: Furthermore, a mendicant lives supported by a town or village.
translation_text.an5.76:10.2: He robes up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, enters a village or town for alms without guarding body, speech, and mind, without establishing mindfulness, and without restraining the sense faculties.
translation_text.an5.76:10.3: There he sees a lady scantily clad, with revealing clothes.
translation_text.an5.76:10.5: and his body and mind burn with it.
translation_text.an5.76:10.7: ‘Why don’t I visit the monastery and tell the monks:
translation_text.an5.76:10.8: “Reverends, I am overcome with lust, mired in lust. I am unable to keep up the spiritual life.
translation_text.an5.76:10.9: I declare my inability to continue training. I disavow it and will return to a lesser life.”’
translation_text.an5.76:10.10: He goes to the monastery and tells the monks:
translation_text.an5.76:10.11: ‘Reverends, I am overcome with lust, mired in lust. I am unable to keep up the spiritual life.
translation_text.an5.76:10.12: I declare my inability to continue training. I disavow it and will return to a lesser life.’
translation_text.an5.76:11.1: His spiritual companions advise and instruct him:
translation_text.an5.76:11.2: ‘Reverend, the Buddha says that sensual pleasures give little gratification and much suffering and distress, and they are all the more full of drawbacks.
translation_text.an5.76:11.3: With the similes of a skeleton …
translation_text.an5.76:11.10: a butcher’s knife and chopping board …
translation_text.an5.76:11.12: a snake’s head, the Buddha says that sensual pleasures give little gratification and much suffering and distress, and they are all the more full of drawbacks.
translation_text.an5.76:11.13: Be happy with the spiritual life.
translation_text.an5.76:11.14: Venerable, please don’t declare your inability to continue training, disavow it and return to a lesser life.’
translation_text.an5.76:12.1: When thus advised and instructed by his spiritual companions, he says:
translation_text.an5.76:12.2: ‘Reverends, even though the Buddha says that sensual pleasures give little gratification and much suffering and distress, and they are all the more full of drawbacks,
translation_text.an5.76:12.3: I am unable to keep up the spiritual life. I declare my inability to continue training. I disavow it and will return to a lesser life.’
translation_text.an5.76:12.4: Declaring his inability to continue training, he disavows it and returns to a lesser life.
translation_text.an5.76:13.1: I say that they are like the warrior who dies of his injuries while in the care of his relatives. Some individuals are like that.
translation_text.an5.76:13.2: This is the third individual similar to a warrior found among the monks.
translation_text.an5.76:14.1: Furthermore, a mendicant lives supported by a town or village.
translation_text.an5.76:14.2: He robes up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, enters a village or town for alms without guarding body, speech, and mind, without establishing mindfulness, and without restraining the sense faculties.
translation_text.an5.76:14.3: There he sees a lady scantily clad, with revealing clothes.
translation_text.an5.76:14.5: and his body and mind burn with it.
translation_text.an5.76:14.7: ‘Why don’t I visit the monastery and tell the monks:
translation_text.an5.76:14.8: “Reverends, I am overcome with lust, mired in lust. I am unable to keep up the spiritual life.
translation_text.an5.76:14.9: I declare my inability to continue training. I disavow it and will return to a lesser life.”’
translation_text.an5.76:14.10: He goes to the monastery and tells the monks:
translation_text.an5.76:14.11: ‘Reverends, I am overcome with lust, mired in lust. I am unable to keep up the spiritual life.
translation_text.an5.76:14.12: I declare my inability to continue training. I disavow it and will return to a lesser life.’
translation_text.an5.76:15.1: His spiritual companions advise and instruct him:
translation_text.an5.76:15.2: ‘Reverend, the Buddha says that sensual pleasures give little gratification and much suffering and distress, and they are all the more full of drawbacks.
translation_text.an5.76:15.3: With the simile of a skeleton …
translation_text.an5.76:15.4: a scrap of meat … a grass torch … a pit of glowing coals … a dream … borrowed goods … fruit on a tree … a butcher’s knife and chopping board … swords and spears … a snake’s head, the Buddha says that sensual pleasures give little gratification and much suffering and distress, and they are all the more full of drawbacks.
translation_text.an5.76:15.5: Be happy with the spiritual life.
translation_text.an5.76:15.6: Venerable, please don’t declare your inability to continue training, disavow it and return to a lesser life.’
translation_text.an5.76:16.1: When thus advised and instructed by his spiritual companions, he says:
translation_text.an5.76:16.2: ‘I’ll try, reverends, I’ll struggle, I’ll be happy.
translation_text.an5.76:16.3: I won’t now declare my inability to continue training, disavow it and return to a lesser life.’
translation_text.an5.76:17.1: I say that this individual is like the warrior who recovers from his injuries while in the care of his relatives.
translation_text.an5.76:17.3: This is the fourth individual similar to a warrior found among the monks.
translation_text.an5.76:18.1: Furthermore, a mendicant lives supported by a town or village.
translation_text.an5.76:18.2: He robes up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, enters a village or town, guarding body, speech, and mind, establishing mindfulness, and restraining the sense faculties.
translation_text.an5.76:18.3: Seeing a sight with his eyes, he doesn’t get caught up in the features and details.
translation_text.an5.76:18.4: If the faculty of sight were left unrestrained, bad unskillful qualities of covetousness and displeasure would become overwhelming. For this reason, he practices restraint, protecting the faculty of sight, and achieving restraint over it.
translation_text.an5.76:18.5: Hearing a sound with his ears …
translation_text.an5.76:18.6: Smelling an odor with his nose …
translation_text.an5.76:18.7: Tasting a flavor with his tongue …
translation_text.an5.76:18.8: Feeling a touch with his body …
translation_text.an5.76:18.9: Knowing an idea with his mind, he doesn’t get caught up in the features and details.
translation_text.an5.76:18.10: If the faculty of mind were left unrestrained, bad unskillful qualities of covetousness and displeasure would become overwhelming. For this reason, he practices restraint, protecting the faculty of mind, and achieving restraint over it.
translation_text.an5.76:18.11: Then after the meal, on his return from almsround,
translation_text.an5.76:18.12: he frequents a secluded lodging—a wilderness, the root of a tree, a hill, a ravine, a mountain cave, a charnel ground, a forest, the open air, a heap of straw.
translation_text.an5.76:18.13: Gone to a wilderness, or to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut, he sits down cross-legged, sets his body straight, and establishes mindfulness in his presence.
translation_text.an5.76:18.14: He gives up these five hindrances, corruptions of the heart that weaken wisdom.
translation_text.an5.76:18.15: Then, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful qualities, he enters and remains in the first absorption … second absorption … third absorption … fourth absorption.
translation_text.an5.76:19.1: When his mind has become immersed in samādhi like this—purified, bright, flawless, rid of corruptions, pliable, workable, steady, and imperturbable—he extends it toward knowledge of the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an5.76:19.2: He truly understands: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’. He truly understands: ‘These are defilements’ … ‘This is the origin of defilements’ … ‘This is the cessation of defilements’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of defilements’. Knowing and seeing like this, his mind is freed from the defilements of sensuality, desire to be reborn, and ignorance. When freed, he knows ‘it is freed’.
translation_text.an5.76:19.3: He understands: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.an5.76:20.1: I say that this individual is like the warrior who dons his sword and shield, fastens his bow and arrows, and plunges into the thick of battle. He wins victory in battle, establishing himself as foremost in battle.
translation_text.an5.76:20.3: This is the fifth individual similar to a warrior found among the monks.
translation_text.an5.76:20.4: These five individuals similar to warriors are found among the monks.”

variant_text.an5.76:8.8: rāgapariyuṭṭhitomhi → rāgāyitomhi (bj, sya-all, km) | sandhāretuṁ → santānetuṁ (bj, sya-all, pts1ed)
